title: PM Sweep Schedule Wiring
objective: Implement the smallest bounded follow-on to packet 019g by wiring the existing `scripts/sweep_pm_idempotency.py` CLI into a deployment-layer scheduled execution path so expired PM idempotency rows are swept automatically without changing application-runtime code, adding DDL, adding PM write endpoints, or reopening `work.wbs_nodes` writes.
constrained_outputs: One bounded deployment-layer schedule artifact that invokes `scripts/sweep_pm_idempotency.py` on a fixed cadence without adding any application-runtime endpoint or background worker
One minimal environment and invocation contract for the scheduled sweep path, including the required working directory, interpreter, and database environment variables
Focused validation evidence proving no application-runtime files changed, `/api/v1/work/*` path count remains 15, and the scheduled sweep path can be invoked using the existing CLI
A bounded handoff naming any residual production deployment work, such as migrating from a scheduled workflow to a Kubernetes CronJob once a deployment-manifests lane exists
validation_steps: Confirm packet 019h stays bounded to deployment-side schedule wiring for the existing sweep CLI and does not modify application-runtime code, add new endpoints, or reopen `work.wbs_nodes` writes
Confirm the scheduled artifact invokes the existing `scripts/sweep_pm_idempotency.py` entrypoint rather than introducing a second sweep implementation path
Confirm no DDL, migration edits, or changes to the `pm.idempotency_keys` schema are introduced
Confirm packet-019g runtime and observability surfaces remain unchanged and that `/api/v1/work/*` path count stays at 15 after the schedule wiring lands
sql_permission_note: Packet 019h is deployment-side only. No new SQL, schema changes, migration edits, runtime route changes, or broader PM write-surface expansion are authorized.
